temporal_description: A man in a dark suit, white shirt, and tie is shown from the waist up, riding a bicycle directly toward the camera. He has a strained and determined expression on his face. The front basket of his bicycle is filled with what appear to be red apples. In the background, a group of cyclists in colorful athletic gear can be seen riding away from the camera down a paved road. The setting is a sunny day in a rural or semi-rural area, with green trees and a clear blue sky. The camera is positioned at a low angle, creating a dynamic and slightly distorted perspective. The audio is filled with an energetic and upbeat rock song. A driving electric guitar riff and a steady, powerful drum beat create a high-energy, motivational atmosphere. A male vocalist sings with a clear, passionate voice, delivering lyrics like, "not enough, not enough for you... not enough for us..." The overall mood of the music is one of determination and forward momentum. The camera angle shifts to a side view, capturing a group of professional cyclists in a tight pack. They are all leaning forward in an aerodynamic position, pedaling intensely. They wear various team jerseys and helmets. The man in the suit is now visible on the far right of the frame, still riding his bicycle, though he is now much smaller in the shot. The background remains a bright, sunny day with a clear sky and a flat landscape. The camera remains stationary, providing a clear view of the cyclists' synchronized movement.
